task: I’m planning a holiday party in Minneapolis and want a quick shortlist of bar caterers that actually look local and usable. Please start on The Knot and look specifically for Minneapolis-area wine or liquor bar service caterers, then open the actual vendor listings in separate tabs so I can compare them visually, and pull together at least five options with each business name and city/state. If Surdyk’s shows up in that shortlist, go to Surdyk’s Catering and look through their site to see whether they feel more full-service than the others, then summarize at least three catering services or service types they offer, including any food and beverage options you can verify on the page, and leave the Surdyk’s page open so I can glance at it later. After that, because I may want a nonalcoholic menu item to pair with whichever caterer seems best, use Google to find a pressure-cooker or Instant Pot mulligatawny soup recipe, open the actual recipe page, and give me the ingredient list plus the basic cooking steps from that one recipe. Please return everything as one concise planning summary so I can compare the caterers, any Surdyk’s details if relevant, and the soup idea all in one place.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched for "Minneapolis wine/liquor/bar service caterers" on The Knot via Google. It identified and opened at least five distinct vendor listings in separate tabs (Hercules Twin City Bartending, With a Twist Bartending Service, Petals and Pints, Knot Your Average Mobile Bartenders LLP, Mix & Mingle Mobile Bar, and Liquidmotion). The agent extracted the business name and city/state for these vendors, as confirmed in the final summary provided in step 34. The verification requirement to confirm that vendor pages were opened is satisfied by the action history (steps 7 and 8).

R2: success - The agent successfully identified that Surdyk’s Catering appeared on The Knot (Step 14). It then navigated to the Surdyk’s Catering website (Step 19) and explored the "Full-Service Catering" (Step 20), "Drop-Off Catering" (Step 21), and "Order Here" (Step 22) pages. The agent's final summary (Step 34) explicitly lists three verified offerings: "Full-service catering for cocktail-style events," "Food service including antipasti stations, hors d’oeuvres, buffet dinners," and "Bar/beverage service with wine, beer, spirits, and signature cocktails." The agent also maintained the Surdyk’s page in the browser tabs throughout the process.

R3: success - The agent successfully searched Google for an "Instant Pot or pressure-cooker mulligatawny soup recipe" (Step 31), opened a relevant recipe page from "Feasting At Home" (Step 32), and included the ingredient list and basic cooking steps in its final summary (Step 34). The information provided is consistent with the recipe found on the opened page.

R4: success - The agent successfully gathered the requested information: a shortlist of Minneapolis-area bar caterers from The Knot, a comparison/summary of Surdyk's Catering (confirming it is more full-service and detailing its offerings), and a mulligatawny soup recipe. The final response is a single, concise planning summary that integrates all these components as requested.
